page: Amazon Web Services c8gn.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor and powered by the 64-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 ARMv9 processor. Operating at 2.8 GHz, this server features 64 dedicated physical cores with a single thread per core, providing 128.0 GB of system memory, which equates to a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor architecture includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. It lacks local NVMe storage and dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core capabilities and exceptional memory throughput. The instance achieves top-tier status in memory bandwidth tests, reaching 7395.26 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 466.64 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Multi-threaded operations perform exceptionally well, as demonstrated by a top-tier bzip3 decompression rate of 661.41 GB/sec and a Geekbench multi-core score of 25974.0. Database and web serving operations also show high efficiency, with Redis SET operations reaching 144.9 million operations per second and static web serving throughput peaking at 116.84 GB/sec. However, single-core CPU performance remains in the average tier, and LLM text generation for small models is relatively weak at 71.03 tokens per second.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c8gn.16xlarge offers high compute density and memory bandwidth per resource unit, making it highly efficient for multi-threaded, network-heavy workloads. The lack of local storage and GPUs represents a tradeoff, directing this instance away from local-storage-dependent databases or GPU-accelerated deep learning. Instead, the hardware profile and benchmark results position this instance for high-throughput network applications, software compilation, multi-threaded data decompression, Redis caching layers, and static web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 64 dedicated physical cores powered by the 2.8 GHz AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 ARMv9 processor. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it provides 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding 2.0 GB of RAM per core, alongside a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. In benchmark evaluations, the c8gn.16xlarge demonstrates top-tier memory bandwidth, reaching 466.64 GB/sec for uncached large blocks, and exceptional multi-threaded performance, including 144.9 million Redis SET operations per second and 116.84 GB/sec static web serving throughput. Single-core performance and small-model LLM text generation represent comparative bottlenecks. This instance is suited for high-throughput network applications, software compilation, multi-threaded decompression, and high-volume web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.16xlarge is a compute-optimized ARM64 instance with 64 Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 128 GB RAM, 200 Gbps network bandwidth, and top-tier multi-threaded benchmark performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.16xlarge is a 64-core ARM64 compute-optimized instance with 128 GB RAM and 200 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A dedicated 64-core ARM64 compute platform delivering 200 Gbps network bandwidth and top-tier multi-threaded memory throughput.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 ARMv9 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz.
128.0 GB of system memory providing a 2.0 GB per core resource density.
High-speed networking with a baseline bandwidth of 200 Gbps.
Top-tier multi-threaded performance in software compilation and Redis operations.
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the hardware profile.
Optimized for static web serving, multi-threaded decompression, and network-heavy workloads.
